title: Srila Prabhupada Stories 3: 120 Chapatis and more
speaker: HG Chanchalapathi Dasa

segments.text: Hare Krishna Krishna Krishna Hare Hare
Hare Rama Hare Rama Rama Rama Hare Hare
Very nice to be with all of you.
We will talk a little bit about Srila Prabhupada's pastimes.
When Srila Prabhupada went to the Western countries for the first time in 1965 and in
the early years, 65, 66 at that time, Srila Prabhupada represented an ancient, pure,
Vedic tradition of Chaitanya Sampradaya and he wanted to share that knowledge and for
the young Americans who were coming to him, he was gradually telling them about philosophy
and some of the principles and one of them was to give up meat eating.
There is no, in the Western countries, there is so much of rampant meat eating and it was
a big thing for people to give up.
In fact, Prabhupada had these four principles, no intoxication, no alcohol, no coffee, tea,
no cigarettes and no meat eating, no gambling and no illicit connection with women.
And in fact, many times, some of the elderly people would say, well, I don't want to eat
There were many people who came and met Srila Prabhupada.
There was one lawyer who was trying to help Srila Prabhupada establish, incorporate the
society and he said, if you have these rules, you won't be able to attract Americans.
Maybe you should not have these rules at all.
And Prabhupada was not ready to compromise.
I have come to, I represent this great tradition from India and I want to present it as it
And if there are, if Americans are not happy with it, I'll go back to my country.
But just for the sake of Americans, just to attract them, I cannot make compromises and
So Srila Prabhupada was very strict about these things.
But one great arsenal Srila Prabhupada had with him, and that was Krishna Prasadam.
So Prabhupada would cook himself in the very early days.
And then these few of the young followers, American men and young boys and girls, and
And some of them were gradually taking it seriously.
But they were, they had given up meat and they were liking the Prasadam that Prabhupada
And you must know Srila Prabhupada was an excellent cook.
And so you can imagine that an excellent cook like Srila Prabhupada, and he prepares, and
then he offers it to Krishna, his dear Lord, what should be the effect, what should be
It was completely out of the world.
And these young Americans, they were actually sold out on that.
And they would eat a lot because, you know, American food, Western food is quite bland,
unlike Indian food, which is very spicy.
And some of them began to like the spicy food and they would eat well.
And one of the devotees recalls that those days there were about 10, 12 of them who ate
There were 10, 12 of them who would come regularly, every day.
There would be more when there were big gatherings.
And these 10, 12 of them would come up to Srila Prabhupada's apartment and Prabhupada would
And then these young American, young American boys and girls, they were very hungry and
they would, they have switched over from all the meat eating and all of that to vegetarian
And Srila Prabhupada would make rotis and chapatis.
And they would, each one would eat about 10 to 12 chapatis.
You know, they were serving hungry.
And Srila Prabhupada was making all of that.
And then the devotees later on, they now, they'll recall that Prabhupada was making
And he would make the dal, he would make the rice, he would make the sabji and all of that
and make it available for the, these young American, these young followers.
His students and they would relish that.
So you can see the kind of effort and the sacrifice that Srila Prabhupada was making.
He is 71 years old at that time.
He's gone from India to America, from Vrindavan.
In fact, those days, I met those young Americans.
They would ask him, what is Vrindavan like?
And Prabhupada would say, I will take you one day and show you Vrindavan.
And you will wonder, how did Swamiji leave Vrindavan and come to this terrible place
This is the way Srila Prabhupada explained.
And so the kind of compassion and sacrifice.
What is the kind of sacrifice Prabhupada was making to spread Krishna Bhakti in a foreign
And so like this, Prabhupada made this very delicious Krishna Prasadam and they would
They would be attracted to Kirtan.
They would be attracted to the philosophy Srila Prabhupada was teaching.
And above all of this, they were drawn and attracted to Srila Prabhupada.
as a person, very compassionate, very learned, taking all the trouble and giving all the time
to explain all the nuances of the philosophy and the doubts that they would have and so they were

and an ashram was set up and Deity was going to be installed. You may remember
that how Srila Prabhupada when initially when devotees went to Africa they had very

several generations, lot of a lot of Indians are there in Nairobi, Mombasa,

So they would give people new information, contemplate and present lies in different religions,
berries, things as a form of sort of self-preservation or self-trust would be remembered.

know how Srila Prabhupada himself went into the kitchen and all these 12

can see that this is a temple, African Nairobi, a temple there, a temple kitchen,
a vegetarian kitchen where Prabhupada is there, sanyasi and he must be around 76
years or so, author of books, rivier sanyasi from India, scholarly and he is
surrounded by 12 black Africans and he is teaching them.
how to do cooking. Can you imagine this? And it was simply because of the drive
that I should help them become devotees of Krishna. I'll tell you one
interesting interaction that I was a witness to maybe about 20 years ago.
There was one quite a senior devotee of ISKCON and I was accompanying him to
Udupi ancient Krishna temple of Madh aula одipi ancient Krishna temple of Madh
bha chariya Sampradayani and then we were interacting with one sanyasi, Madhva
sanyasi, and this senior devotee who was with me from ISKCON and he was
talking to him and I was trying to translate that to the language that the
sanyasi can understand and so this devotee said Swamiji you must come to America to read this scriptures, let's cutخ

Slavim ye the target of your avatars and to the detail of what have you dust and to understand and so this devotee said Swamiji you must come to America, please girl, you must come to America, too and then came along.

Swamiji, you must come to America, there is immense preaching field, lot of people want to hear about Krishna and you must come and you are all very learned sanyasis of the Madhva Sampradaya, you are all Vaishnavas.

You must come and teach about Krishna to Americans and rest of the world. And so that sanyasi, Madhva sanyasi, very elderly sanyasi, very respectable sanyasi, he said, you see if we have to come, every day, every place, we will need well water.
If there is no well water, we cannot come there, we cannot come out, do our cooking, we cannot come out.
We cannot do our puja, we cannot come to such place. So I was reflecting, you just see the contrast.
Srila Prabhupada has gone to places like New York, Africa and innumerable other western cities and Prabhupada, surrounded by these westerners, he has convinced them to become vegetarians.
He is teaching them how to cook Indian food, how to offer to Krishna.
How to make it Krishna Prasadam, change their lifestyle.
Look at the compassion, look at the sacrifice that Srila Prabhupada is making to spread Krishna consciousness all over the world.
Compared to, you know, of course they are all very respectable sadhus are there in our country, very respectable sanyasis, sadhus and different mats and different organizations are there.
In this, at such an advanced stage, the kind of sacrifice that Srila Prabhupada is making, this is something, you know, extraordinary that Srila Prabhupada did.
I must share another very interesting conversation that happened among the devotees in our temple here in Bangalore, just a few months ago.
Bangalore temple devotees, we were all talking.
And then we strayed off into talking a few other topics other than the main topics in the meeting, formal temple meeting.
The discussion came up about one other spiritual organization from our country, very well known.
They also have temples around the world and a lot of followers.
And then they also have a lot of their sadhus, they call them followers, their sadhus.
And then the discussion came up that how these...
These sadhus of this organization, I don't want to mention the organization, very respectable, nice people, well known to us also.
And these sadhus of that organization, they are very austere.
And then we were told that, we learned that what they do, they have a feast and they offer that on special days, you know, like Ram Navami or something.
They have their offering and they make a feast.
And finally, the sadhus in the ashram.
The sadhus in the ashram, what they do is that there's a big feast.
Now they mix all the feasts, daal, sabji, gulab jamun, khichdi, shaag, bonda, pakoda, whatever.
Everything, they mix it up, mash it up like that.
And then pour water and then drink it.
So that, you know, they don't enjoy the feast.
They enjoy their senses eating good food or something like that.
So, when we were talking about this, one of the devotees in our meeting group said,
Wow, these sadhus, they are so austere.
Oh my God, they just can't even have a feast.
And they do this every day.
And they have a bowl, supposedly.
And whatever it fills in that bowl, only that much they can eat.
They can't even take more than that.
And you have to, this kind of a mashed food you have to eat every day.
Oh my God, what an austerity.
And then this one, one of the devotees who heard this very spontaneously said,
My God, next time I see these sadhus, I'll offer my pranams to them and tell them,
Wow, hats off to the austerity that you are practicing.
Look at what Srila Prabhupada said.
Srila Prabhupada has offered us a spiritual mind where we can,
we also make very nice elaborate feast, offer to the Lord.
And we also relish Krishna Prasadam with all of the very gatedness.
And after having enjoyed this wonderful Krishna Prasadam,
Srila Prabhupada is offering us a state of consciousness far, far higher than all of these other sadhus
This is what the Acharyas have explained.
This is the Krishna Bhakti Rasa that Chaitanya Mahaprabhu is teaching is so extraordinary.
It is the highest aspects of Krishna Bhakti.
That and also a delicious meal.
So, if you are saying that you have to offer obeisance or pranams to those sadhus,
what you should do to Srila Prabhupada?
And everyone said, yes, we should offer our lives to Srila Prabhupada.
And even that's not enough to repay what Srila Prabhupada has given us.

was teaching around the world was something,
